name: Pizza Dough (Penny)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine 2 cups of flour, yeast, and salt.
2. In a measuring cup, mix the water, olive oil, and honey. Stir into the flour mixture to form a soft dough. Turn out onto a floured surface and knead with the remaining 1/2 cup of flour until the dough is very elastic (about 10 minutes). The dough should be soft; add flour gradually and only as needed.
3. Wash, dry, and lightly oil the mixing bowl. Place the dough in the bowl, turning to coat all sides. Cover with plastic wrap and a towel, and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size (30-45 minutes). If more time is available, allow it to rise at room temperature for up to 2 hours; if it rises longer, punch it down and let it rise again.
4. Shape the dough, top as desired, and bake in an oven set to 400°F for about 12-15 minutes. Baking on a baking stone is recommended if available.
5. For the sauce, use Contadina from a squeeze bottle, then sprinkle extra oregano, basil, and thyme before adding other toppings.
6. You can substitute half a cup of flour with white whole wheat flour for added flavor; more than that will make the dough very stiff. When substituting with wheat flour, add an extra teaspoon of honey.
